label: Motivation (SVD-LLM limitations)
verbatim: SVD-LLM has two limitations. First, SVD-LLM applies a homogeneous compression ratio to all the weight matrices. This coarse-grained setup unfortunately overlooks the heterogeneity of weight redundancy across different LLM layers. Second, SVD-LLM utilizes Cholesky decomposition for weight truncation. However, Cholesky decomposition requires the matrix being decomposed to be positive-definite, a condition that is challenging to fulfill in practice. Moreover, Cholesky decomposition introduces numerical instability throughout its iterative process.
paraphrase: SVD-LLM (V1) has two flaws: homogeneous compression ratio across layers ignores heterogeneous redundancy; and the Cholesky decomposition step is numerically unstable and requires positive-definiteness that is not always satisfied.
preconditions: Comparison vs SVD-LLM V1 (Wang et al. 2024b)
consequences: Motivates per-type rank allocation and SVD-twice truncation in V2.

label: V2 contributions
verbatim: First, SVD-LLM V2 proposes to use theoretical truncation loss of weight matrices to assign a unique compression ratio to each weight matrix at different layers to accommodate weight redundancy heterogeneity. Second, SVD-LLM V2 proposes loss-optimized weight truncation to ensure that the truncated singular values result in a lower and more stable truncation loss in practice.
paraphrase: Two contributions: (1) per-matrix compression ratio guided by theoretical truncation loss; (2) loss-optimized weight truncation replacing Cholesky.
consequences: Defines the two algorithmic components of V2.

label: Eq. 1
verbatim: L = ||W X − W'X||_F
paraphrase: Truncation loss is the Frobenius norm of (W − W')X, where W is original, W' is the compressed low-rank weight, X is calibration activation.
preconditions: Calibration activation X for layer; SVD low-rank approximation W'.
consequences: Defines target loss minimized by all SVD-based methods including V2.

label: Per-type grouping
verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 groups the weight matrices across all the layers in the original LLM by type, such as query (Q) and key (K) in attention blocks, and Gate (G) and Up (U) in MLP blocks. It then computes the theoretical truncation loss of the weight matrices and assigns a unique compression ratio to each weight matrix within each group based on the computed truncation loss.
paraphrase: V2 groups weight matrices by projection type (Q, K, V/O implied; Gate, Up, Down implied) across all layers, then assigns a per-matrix ratio inside each group from the theoretical truncation loss.
preconditions: Standard transformer block with Q, K, V, O attention projections and Gate, Up, Down MLP projections.
consequences: Per-type rank allocation: ratios differ across types and across layers within a type.

label: Algorithm 1
verbatim: procedure RATIO_ALLOCATION(M, S, R): G ← Group(M); Rd ← ∅; for g in G do: LG ← ∅; for w in g do: Lmin ← Theoretical_Loss(w, x, R); LG ← LG ∪ Lmin; end for; LG ← 1/Log(LG); r ← Len(LG) × R × Lmin/Sum(LG); Rd ← Rd ∪ r; end for; return Rd
paraphrase: Heterogeneous Compression Ratio Allocation: group weights by type; for each weight in the group, compute its theoretical minimum truncation loss Lmin at uniform ratio R; transform via 1/log(L); per-matrix ratio r = |G|·R·Lmin / Σ_G L_normalized.
preconditions: Original LLM M, calibration activation x, target overall ratio R.
consequences: Returns a per-matrix ratio list Rd respecting target R while reallocating budget across types/layers.

label: Alg. 1 line 11
verbatim: r ← Len(LG) × R × Lmin/Sum(LG)
paraphrase: Per-matrix ratio = (group size) × (target ratio R) × Lmin / Sum(LG), where LG is the (1/log) normalized list of theoretical truncation losses in the group.
preconditions: LG is normalized as LG ← 1/Log(LG) before this line.
consequences: Ratio is proportional to the matrix's normalized loss share within its type group.

label: Theoretical truncation loss formula
verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 computes the theoretical minimum truncation loss of the weight matrices, i.e., Lmin = ||C − C'||_F, where C is the original matrix of W X and C' is its compressed version by SVD, respectively. It then inverses and normalizes Lmin by 1/Log(Lmin).
paraphrase: Lmin is computed as ||WX − (WX)_rank-k||_F where rank-k truncation is applied directly to WX (the activation-multiplied matrix). Then loss is normalized by 1/Log(Lmin).
preconditions: Calibration data X; rank corresponding to ratio R.
consequences: Provides a cheap proxy that bypasses end-to-end perplexity evaluation (ASVD-style).

label: Efficiency vs ASVD
verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 bypasses the need to measure end-to-end perplexity to determine compression ratios, as done in ASVD and is time-consuming. Instead, it utilizes truncation loss, which is easy to obtain and thereby enhancing the efficiency of the algorithm.
paraphrase: Per-matrix ratio is determined from cheap-to-compute truncation loss rather than ASVD's expensive end-to-end PPL evaluation.
consequences: V2 ratio search is fast (~minutes) while assigning per-matrix non-uniform ranks.

label: Figure 3 motivation
verbatim: we use SVD-LLM to measure the truncation loss of the query matrix across different layers in LLaMA-3 8B on WikiText-2 dataset with 50% compression ratio. As shown in Figure 3, the truncation loss varies at different layers. For example, the query matrix in the 27th layer has a much higher truncation loss than that of the first layer, indicating the 27th layer should be compressed under a smaller compression ratio, while a larger compression ratio should be applied to the first layer.
paraphrase: Empirical evidence: truncation loss of LLaMA-3 8B query matrix at 50% ratio varies layer-to-layer; layer 27 has much higher loss than layer 1, motivating per-layer per-type ratios.
preconditions: LLaMA-3 8B, WikiText-2, 50% ratio, query matrix only.
consequences: Justifies non-uniform per-projection rank allocation.

label: Explicit type list
verbatim: given that different types of weight matrices, such as query (Q) and key (K) in attention blocks, and Gate (G) and Up (U) in MLP blocks play different roles in an LLM, SVD-LLM V2 first groups the weight matrices across all the layers in the original LLM according to their types.
paraphrase: Grouping is explicitly per projection type (Q, K in attention; Gate, Up in MLP). The paper exemplifies but does not enumerate all 7 LLaMA projections; V/O/Down are implicit in the same scheme.
preconditions: LLaMA-style architecture with Q/K/V/O + Gate/Up/Down projections.
consequences: Groups in Algorithm 1 = projection-type buckets, NOT per-layer buckets.

label: Algorithm 2
verbatim: procedure WEIGHT_TRUNCATION(W, X, R): S ← XX^T; Us, Ss, Vs ← SVD(S); D ← W × Us × √Ss; Uws, Sws, Vws ← SVD(D); Ts ← Truncate(Sws, R); W' ← Uws × Ts × S^{-1}_s × U^{-1}_s; return W'
paraphrase: Loss-optimized weight truncation: (1) S = XX^T; (2) SVD(S) → Us, Ss, Vs; (3) D = W·Us·√Ss; (4) SVD(D) → Uws, Sws, Vws; (5) Ts = truncate Sws at ratio R; (6) W' = Uws · Ts · √Ss^{-1} · Us^{-1}.
preconditions: Calibration activation X; weight W; per-matrix ratio R from Alg. 1.
consequences: Replaces Cholesky in SVD-LLM V1 with two SVDs; numerically more stable; achieves theoretical Lmin.

label: Theorem 3.1
verbatim: If Us, Ss, Vs are obtained by SVD decomposition of XX^T and Uws, Sws, Vws are obtained by SVD decomposition of W × Us × √Ss, the compressed weight matrix W' = Uws × Trunc.(Sws) × Vws × √Ss^{-1} × Us^{-1} ensures the theoretical minimum truncation loss.
paraphrase: Theorem: the V2 two-SVD construction attains the theoretical minimum Frobenius truncation loss ||WX − W'X||_F at the chosen rank.
preconditions: X with SVD Ux, Sx, Vx; XX^T symmetric so Us=Ux and √Ss=Sx.
consequences: Establishes optimality of V2 truncation, matching SVD-LLM V1's theoretical guarantee but without Cholesky.

label: Eq. 2
paraphrase: Key identity used in proof: S^{-1}X equals V_x (right singular matrix of X).
consequences: Lets Frobenius norm be invariant under multiplication by S^{-1}X (orthogonal columns).

label: Eq. 3
verbatim: L^2 = ||WX − W'X||_F^2 = ||WSS^{-1}X − Uws·Trunc.(Sws)·Vws·S^{-1}X||_F^2 = ||(WS − Uws·Trunc.(Sws)·Vws)S^{-1}X||_F^2 = ||WS − Uws·Trunc.(Sws)·Vws||_F^2 = ||SVD(WS)||_F^2 = ||SVD(W·Ux·Sx)||_F^2 = ||SVD(W·Ux·Sx·Vx)||_F^2 = ||SVD(WX)||_F^2 = L_min^2
paraphrase: Closed-form derivation that V2 truncation loss equals L_min, the SVD truncation loss of WX.
consequences: Proves V2 attains theoretical minimum truncation loss.

label: Cholesky failure mode
verbatim: because XX^T is not positive definite, SVD-LLM fails to compress matrix A. For matrix B, even when the compression ratio is as low as 20%, SVD-LLM still achieves a larger truncation loss in practice than in theory, and this difference even increases with increasing compression ratio.
paraphrase: On randomly selected LLaMA-3 8B matrices A and B with 256 C4 calibration samples: SVD-LLM (V1) Cholesky outright fails when XX^T is not PD (matrix A), and shows growing practical-vs-theoretical loss gap at higher ratios (matrix B).
consequences: Justifies replacing Cholesky with two-SVD.

label: Table 1, A@20%
verbatim: MATRIX A RATIO 20%: Theoretical 0.5982; SVD-LLM Fail; SVD-LLM V2 0.5982
paraphrase: Matrix A at 20%: theoretical loss 0.5982; SVD-LLM V1 fails; V2 attains 0.5982 (matches theory).
consequences: V2 hits theoretical minimum where V1 cannot run.

label: Table 1, A@60%
verbatim: MATRIX A RATIO 60%: Theoretical 2.3251; SVD-LLM Fail; SVD-LLM V2 2.3251
paraphrase: Matrix A at 60%: theoretical 2.3251; V1 fails; V2 = 2.3251.
consequences: Same.

label: Table 1, B@20%
verbatim: MATRIX B RATIO 20%: Theoretical 0.7351; SVD-LLM 0.8961; SVD-LLM V2 0.7351 (↓18%)
paraphrase: Matrix B at 20%: theoretical 0.7351; V1 0.8961; V2 0.7351 (18% lower than V1).
consequences: Quantifies V2 advantage from numerical stability.

label: Table 1, B@60%
verbatim: MATRIX B RATIO 60%: Theoretical 3.5245; SVD-LLM 5.9834; SVD-LLM V2 3.5245 (↓41%)
paraphrase: Matrix B at 60%: theoretical 3.5245; V1 5.9834; V2 3.5245 (41% lower than V1).
consequences: V1 gap grows with ratio; V2 stays at theoretical.

label: Calibration set
verbatim: We randomly select 256 WikiText-2 samples as the calibration data.
paraphrase: Default calibration: 256 WikiText-2 samples.
consequences: Determines X for both Lmin computation and weight truncation.

label: Noise injection (V1 baseline)
verbatim: To mitigate the error raised by the Choleksy decomposition in SVD-LLM due to positive definite, we followed the implementation of SVD-LLM (Wang et al., 2024b) to add the small noise into the decomposed matrices.
paraphrase: When running SVD-LLM V1 baseline, small noise is added to decomposed matrices to handle non-PD case (per V1's recipe).
consequences: Note: V2 itself does not need this noise.

label: Compression ratio definition
verbatim: The compression ration in our experiments refers to the parameter reduction of LLM achieved through compression.
paraphrase: Compression ratio = fraction of parameters removed from the LLM.
consequences: 20% ratio means 20% parameter reduction (rank such that param count drops by 20%).

label: Hardware
verbatim: All of the experiments are conducted on A100 GPUs.
paraphrase: All experiments run on NVIDIA A100 GPUs.
consequences: Speed/throughput numbers benchmark on A100.

label: Models evaluated
verbatim: we evaluate the performance of SVD-LLM V2 on five models at various scales (LLaMA-7B, 13B, 30B, LLaMA3-8B (Touvron et al., 2023), OPT-6.7B (Zhang et al., 2022))
paraphrase: Five models: LLaMA-7B, LLaMA-13B, LLaMA-30B, LLaMA-3 8B, OPT-6.7B.
consequences: Generalizability claim across families and scales.

label: Datasets evaluated
verbatim: ten datasets including two language modeling datasets (WikiText-2, C4), six classification datasets (OpenbookQA, WinoGrande, HellaSwag, Arc_e, PIQA, MathQA), and two generation datasets (TruthfulQA and GSM8K) with the LM-Evaluation-Harness framework
paraphrase: 10 datasets: 2 LM (WikiText-2, C4), 6 classification (OpenbookQA, WinoGrande, HellaSwag, ARC-e, PIQA, MathQA), 2 generation (TruthfulQA, GSM8K) via lm-eval-harness.
consequences: Defines evaluation protocol.

label: SVD baselines
verbatim: Three state-of-the-art SVD-based LLM compression methods: FWSVD (Hsu et al., 2022), ASVD (Yuan et al., 2023), and SVD-LLM (Wang et al., 2024b)
paraphrase: SVD baselines: FWSVD, ASVD, SVD-LLM (V1).
consequences: Defines reference set.

label: Table 2, LLaMA-7B
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B at 20% ratio: V2 PPL WikiText-2=7.12 (vs V1 7.94, −10%), C4=10.47 (vs V1 15.84, −34%); avg classification acc 0.54 vs V1 0.52.
consequences: Headline win on LLaMA-7B.

label: Table 2, OPT-6.7B
consequences: Generalizes to OPT family.

label: Table 2, LLaMA-3 8B
consequences: Largest win on the most-modern model.

label: Table 3, LLaMA-13B
consequences: Scales to 13B.

label: Table 3, LLaMA-30B
consequences: Scales to 30B.

label: Compression speed
verbatim: FWSVD takes about 6 GPU hours, ASVD takes about 5.5 GPU hours, SVD-LLM takes about 15 minutes, and SVD-LLM V2 takes about 18 minutes to finish the compression.
paraphrase: On LLaMA-7B at 20%: FWSVD ~6 A100-h; ASVD ~5.5 A100-h; SVD-LLM V1 ~15 min; SVD-LLM V2 ~18 min.
consequences: V2 adds only ~3 min over V1 despite per-matrix ratio search.

label: Inference speedup
verbatim: speedup of 1.29x under 20% compression ratio, 1.63x under 40% compression ratio, 2.08x under 60% compression ratio, and 2.71x under 80% compression ratio.
paraphrase: On LLaMA-7B compressed by V2 vs original on a single A100 (batch=4, prefill=1024, decode=256): 1.29x@20%, 1.63x@40%, 2.08x@60%, 2.71x@80%.
consequences: Hardware-friendly compression.

label: Table 4 ablation
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B 20% Wiki PPL: V1 7.94; V2(A) (allocation only) 7.91; V2(T) (truncation only) 7.43; V2 (both) 7.12.
consequences: Both components individually beat V1; combined is best. Truncation contributes more (-6%) than allocation (-1%) alone.

label: Calibration robustness
verbatim: the changes in both data number and sampling seed in the calibration set incur no more than 1% fluctuation in the final performance, demonstrating that SVD-LLM V2 is not sensitive to the calibration set.
paraphrase: Sweeping #samples ∈ {256, 512, 1024, 2048} and seeds ∈ {3, 17, 42, 100} on WikiText-2 LLaMA-7B 20% changes PPL by ≤1%.
consequences: V2 is robust to calibration choice.

label: Figure 6
verbatim: Number of data: 256 / 512 / 1024 / 2048; Perplexity range 7.12-7.24. Sampling seed: 3 / 17 / 42 / 100; Perplexity range 7.06-7.15.
paraphrase: PPL on LLaMA-7B@20% varies from ~7.12-7.24 across calibration sizes 256-2048 and ~7.06-7.15 across seeds {3,17,42,100}.
consequences: Quantifies <1% sensitivity claim.

label: Table 5 vs structured pruning
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B PPL on Wiki at memory budgets 10/9/8/7 GB: V2 dominates LLM-Pruner, SliceGPT, BlockPruner; gap reaches 49% at 8 GB.
consequences: V2 beats SOTA structured pruning at matched memory.

label: Table 6 average accuracy
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B average classification accuracy at 10/9/8/7 GB: V2 wins by 2/6/11/13 pp respectively over best pruning baseline.
consequences: Same.

label: Table 7 vs GPTQ
verbatim: GPTQ-3bit 2.8 GB 16.28; SVD-LLM V2 2.8 GB 119; SVD-LLM V2 + GPTQ-4bit 2.8 GB 9.97 (↓39%)
paraphrase: LLaMA-7B Wiki PPL at 2.8 GB: GPTQ-3bit=16.28; V2 alone=119; V2 (30% ratio) + GPTQ-4bit=9.97 (39% better than GPTQ-3bit).
consequences: Compose SVD-LLM V2 with quantization for synergy.

label: Table 8 vs 1-bit quant
verbatim: PB-LLM 1-bit 1.9 GB 104.83; BiLLM 1-bit 1.5 GB 47.67; SVD-LLM V2 16-bit 1.5 GB 99.64; SVD-LLM V2 2-bit 1.5 GB 14.73 (↓69%)
paraphrase: At 1.5 GB on LLaMA-7B Wiki: V2-16bit 99.64 < PB-LLM-1bit 104.83 (5% better); V2-2bit 14.73 (69% lower than BiLLM-1bit 47.67).
consequences: V2 + 2-bit quant beats 1-bit baselines.

label: Throughput claim
verbatim: LLMs compressed by SVD-LLM V2 are able to achieve a throughput speedup of up to 2.71× compared to the original LLMs on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU.
paraphrase: Up to 2.71× throughput speedup on A100 (at 80% ratio).
consequences: Headline inference acceleration claim.

label: SVD+quant orthogonality
verbatim: by combining with 2-bit quantization, SVD-LLM V2 is able to outperform 1-bit BiLLM, demonstrating the promise of combining SVD and quantization-based methods
paraphrase: SVD compression and quantization are orthogonal; combining V2 with 2-bit quantization beats 1-bit BiLLM at the same memory budget.
consequences: Validates stacking SVD+quant pipelines.

label: Limitation at 90%
verbatim: under 90% compression ratio, there is a small performance gap compared with state-of-the-art quantization methods.
paraphrase: At very aggressive 90% compression ratio, V2 still trails SOTA quantization methods.
consequences: Acknowledged residual gap.

label: Group semantics: per-type across-layers
verbatim: groups the weight matrices across all the layers in the original LLM by type ... assigns a unique compression ratio to each weight matrix within each group based on the computed truncation loss.
paraphrase: Group = projection type (e.g., all Q matrices across all N layers form one group). Inside the group, each per-layer matrix gets its own ratio. The mean within-group ratio equals R but per-layer ratios vary.
consequences: Spec implication: rank allocation is per-(type, layer) pair, NOT a single ratio per type.

label: Loss inversion 1/log
verbatim: LG ← 1/Log(LG) ▷ Normalize LG
paraphrase: Per-group losses are transformed by f(L) = 1 / log(L) before being used in the ratio formula. Higher loss → smaller normalized value → smaller r → less compression. Inverse mapping ensures higher-loss matrices get more rank budget.
preconditions: L computed via SVD on WX.
consequences: Defines exact non-uniform allocation rule. Note: log without specified base; signs assume L>1.

label: Two SVDs replace Cholesky
verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 bypasses the Cholesky decomposition, resulting in a more straightforward process with improved numerical stability. Specifically, given the input activation X, SVD-LLM V2 conducts SVD on XX^T to obtain the decomposed matrices Us, Ss, Vs ... It then conducts another round of SVD on W × Us × √Ss to obtain Uws, Sws, Vws.
paraphrase: V2 truncation = SVD(XX^T) followed by SVD(W·Us·√Ss). No Cholesky.
consequences: Numerically stable; works when XX^T is only PSD (not strictly PD).

label: Final reconstruction formula
verbatim: The final compressed weight matrix W' can be obtained via Uws × Trunc.(Sws) × S_s^{-1} × U_s^{-1}
paraphrase: W' = Uws · Trunc(Sws) · √Ss^{-1} · Us^{-1}. (Theorem 3.1 statement also includes Vws factor: W' = Uws · Trunc(Sws) · Vws · √Ss^{-1} · Us^{-1}.) The Vws factor appears in the theorem; algorithm pseudocode omits it (likely typo or absorbed).
preconditions: Algorithm 2 outputs Uws, Sws, Vws and Us, Ss.
consequences: Spec implementations should follow Theorem 3.1's full form including Vws.

label: XX^T symmetric PSD
verbatim: Since XX^T is the symmetric matrix, suppose that the singular vectors and values of input activation X is Ux, Sx, Vx, we have Us = Ux and √Ss = Sx.
paraphrase: XX^T is symmetric PSD with eigendecomposition Us·Ss·Us^T; Us = Ux (left singular vectors of X) and √Ss = Sx (singular values of X).
consequences: Connects XX^T's SVD to X's SVD; foundation of proof.

label: Spec motivation cite (per-projection rank bias)
verbatim: the truncation loss varies at different layers ... indicating the 27th layer should be compressed under a smaller compression ratio, while a larger compression ratio should be applied to the first layer.
paraphrase: Per-projection rank-bias motivation: empirical evidence that uniform ratio is suboptimal; some layers/types tolerate aggressive compression while others need preservation.
preconditions: LLaMA-3 8B query matrix loss profile across 32 layers.
consequences: This is the canonical reference cited by the moe_compress spec for D7a per-projection rank bias.

label: Ablation calibration sweep
verbatim: Number of data: 256 512 1024 2048 ... Seed for Random Sampling: 3 17 42 100
paraphrase: Calibration ablation tested {256, 512, 1024, 2048} samples and seeds {3, 17, 42, 100}.
consequences: Defines the robustness sweep ranges.

label: Orthogonality claim
verbatim: SVD-LLM V2 is orthogonal to other post-training LLM compression methods, including quantization and pruning.
paraphrase: V2 is orthogonal to quantization and pruning methods.
consequences: Justifies stacking with GPTQ etc.

label: Loss compression by log
verbatim: It then inverses and normalizes Lmin by 1/Log(Lmin).
paraphrase: Loss is reduced to a slowly-varying scale via reciprocal of natural log; this prevents one extreme-loss matrix from dominating the budget allocation.
consequences: Smooths the per-matrix allocation; matrices with very high Lmin still get more rank but not pathologically more.

label: No PPL search needed
verbatim: existing SVD-based LLM compression methods either overlook this variation or require resource-intensive operations to determine the specific compression ratios, making them impractical for compressing LLMs at larger scales.
paraphrase: ASVD and similar methods need expensive PPL search; this is impractical at scale. V2's truncation-loss-only allocation is cheap.
consequences: Practical efficiency argument for V2's allocation rule.
